subject: chemistry
title: 22M.1.SL.TZ2.14
question_html:
Which combination of ΔH1, ΔH2, and ΔH3 would give the enthalpy of the reaction?

CS2 (l) + 3O2 (g) → CO2 (g) + 2SO2 (g)

ΔH1  C (s) + O2 (g) → CO2 (g)
ΔH2  S (s) + O2 (g) → SO2 (g)
ΔH3  C (s) + 2S (s) → CS2 (l)

A.  ΔH = ΔH1 + ΔH2 + ΔH3

B.  ΔH = ΔH1 + ΔH2 − ΔH3

C.  ΔH = ΔH1 + 2(ΔH2) + ΔH3

D.  ΔH = ΔH1 + 2(ΔH2) − ΔH3
examiners_html: A very well answered question using Hess’s law. 85% of the candidates used the enthalpies given to calculate the unknown enthalpy of reaction.
